nativeRange.habitat: Shallow exposed reef slopes and reef crests with strong water movement; generally found in high-light, high-flow upper reef zones

decorPreferences: stable base with spacing from neighbors

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Supplemental target feeding of micro-plankton, rotifers, and brine shrimp nauplii is generally advised on a weekly basis to support tissue health and growth. Calcium, strontium, and trace element supplementation are recommended.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals placed in direct contact

summary: Stylophora is a branching SPS coral with thick, blunt-tipped branches widely regarded as one of the more beginner-friendly SPS corals. Requires stable reef conditions with moderate-to-high light and strong flow.
careNotes:
Note: Stylophora is a genus of 14 recognized species commonly sold at the genus level in the aquarium trade. Stylophora pistillata is the most widely studied and commonly exported species, but trade frags may represent any species. The scientificName field is set to 'Stylophora sp.' to reflect this trade convention.

Stylophora is generally considered one of the more forgiving SPS corals, making it a popular entry point for hobbyists moving from soft and LPS corals to small polyp stony corals. Its thick, blunt-branched structure is more resilient than finer-branched SPS like Acropora.

Key care requirements:
• Lighting: Moderate-to-high intensity is generally advised, typically in the 150–300 PAR range. New specimens should be acclimated gradually — start at lower light levels and raise intensity over 2–4 weeks to prevent bleaching.
• Flow: Strong, intermittent (alternating) circulation is recommended. Direct laminar flow tends to be less ideal than a chaotic or gyre-type pattern. Branches should sway gently but not be overwhelmed.
• Water chemistry: Calcium, alkalinity (dKH), and magnesium should be maintained consistently. Calcium targets of 400–450 ppm, alkalinity of 8–12 dKH, and magnesium of 1250–1400 ppm are commonly cited for SPS systems. Swings in alkalinity can cause bleaching or tissue necrosis.
• Placement: Middle to upper regions of the aquarium are generally recommended to maximize light exposure while keeping the colony accessible for observation.
• Aggression: Stylophora is considered peaceful and does not have powerful sweeper tentacles, but physical contact with neighboring corals should be avoided. Maintain adequate spacing to allow for colony growth.
• Colony colors: Trade frags are available in green, pink, purple, yellow ('Butternut'), and multi-color ('Rainbow') morphs. Color is influenced by both lighting spectrum and nutrient levels.
• Fragging: Responds well to fragging with bone cutters or scissors. Frags encrust and resume growth readily on frag plugs or rubble.
• Pests: Generally less susceptible to Acropora-eating flatworms (AEFW) than Acropora, but dipping new acquisitions (e.g., CoralRx) is still a good practice.
• Conservation: Stylophora pistillata is listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List; most trade specimens are aquacultured.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. Colonies respond well to fragmentation and can be propagated with standard fragging tools; frags as small as 1/2 inch typically encrust and recover well. Sexual reproduction occurs on natural reefs via broadcast spawning, but deliberate captive sexual breeding is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page for Pink Stylophora Coral. Confirmed scientific name Stylophora spp., temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level Moderate, flow strong intermittent, placement middle to top, feeding supplemental micro-plankton and brine shrimp.

additional.name: ReefChasers – Stylophora Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Confirmed beginner-friendly SPS, PAR 300–400 (upper end; task brief specifies 150–300 PAR given forgiving nature; 150–300 adopted as conservative range covering typical aquarium setups), flow moderately strong alternating, peaceful temperament with no aggressive defenses, supplemental feeding with rotifers and vitamins. No exact water parameter numbers provided.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Stylophora pistillata
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Family: Pocilloporidae, Order: Scleractinia), native range (Red Sea, Indo-Pacific), depth range (to ~15 m), and colony size (max ~30 cm diameter for S. pistillata). Conservation status: Near Threatened (IUCN). ~10,000 specimens exported for reef trade in 2005.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Stylophora (coral genus)
additional.notes: Confirmed 14 recognized species in genus, common names (Cat's paw corals, birdsnest corals), branching structure with blunt-tipped finger-like branches. Color range: orange, pink, magenta, purple, green, brown. Distribution includes Red Sea through Pitcairn Islands.
